purpose: SpecDetect spectral descriptors of the per-token surprisal vector (Yang et al. 2025, arXiv:2508.11343). Takes the log-probability sequence a single causal LM already produces and reads its DFT magnitude spectrum: spectral centroid, low-frequency energy fraction, spectral flatness, peak frequency bin, and dominant period in tokens — a measurement of the sequential/spectral structure of the predictability signal that the DivEye point moments (mean/SD/lag-k ACF) don't capture. Orthogonal to Binoculars' cross-perplexity ratio, Fast-DetectGPT curvature, and the DivEye moments by construction (the magnitude spectrum is the whole second-order structure; a single lag-k ACF is one projection of it). Lastde / Lastde++ diversity-entropy (Xu et al. 2024, arXiv:2410.06072) ships as a library function but is NOT emitted by this surface at M1 — it is gated on an in-tree orthogonality check (M2). M1 is SpecDetect-only, stdlib, model-free over an injected sequence.
use_when: You want a sequential/spectral read of the surprisal vector distinct from the DivEye point moments (a whole magnitude spectrum, not one lag-k autocorrelation)
You're stacking orthogonal Surface-5 signals (Binoculars + curvature + DivEye + spectral)
Target is long enough for a stable spectrum (≥ 50 words / 30 scored tokens)
do_not_use_when: You need a binary verdict — ships uncalibrated by default, no threshold; bands are PROVISIONAL and name a spectrum property, not an AI/human class
The prose is formulaic, translated, or ESL — these produce regular spectra too (a concentrated spectrum is not an AI finding; a flat one is not a human finding)
You need to compare across runs with different models/checkpoints/dtypes (the spectrum is tokenizer- and model-bound)
You can't install the surprisal tier (transformers + torch) for a real scored run

compute.cost_note: reuses the surprisal series score_text already returns (no extra forward pass); the spectral core is stdlib arithmetic

examples.description: Custom model + low-frequency cutoff, JSON envelope
